Video.summary: What good is the warmth of summer, without the cold of the Winter.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 4.10

Video.summary: Far from the reaches of human society, the animal kingdom thrives.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.2

Video.summary: What good is the warmth of summer, without the cold of the Winter.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 4.10

Video.summary: Far from the reaches of human society, the animal kingdom thrives.
Video.title: WS Viral Wild | Ep. 1.2

Video.summary: Mark and Jaclyn make their annual trek to Tornado Alley and this time they are looking for more than severe thunderstorms and tornadoes. They also want answers as to whether Climate Change is causing changes to Tornado Alley.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Shifting Winds

Video.summary: George joins Mark and Jaclyn on a journey to Nepal.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1

Image.alt: Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1
Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1
Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1

Video.summary: Mark and Jaclyn make their annual trek to Tornado Alley and this time they are looking for more than severe thunderstorms and tornadoes. They also want answers as to whether Climate Change is causing changes to Tornado Alley.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Shifting Winds

Video.summary: George joins Mark and Jaclyn on a journey to Nepal.
Video.title: Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1

Image.alt: Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1
Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1
Storm Hunters | Height of Ruin - Part 1

Video.summary: George investigates the wild side of water in dangerous class 5 river rapids.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water

Video.summary: Mount Washington, New Hampshire has the dubious title of being home to the world’s worst weather. The highest wind ever recorded on the planet – 231 miles per hour – was recorded at its mountaintop observatory. To prepare for a mid-winter assault on the summit, Angry Planet host George Kourounis becomes a “human popsicle”, encased in ice in at the National Resarch Council’s high speed wind tunnel. Then he and his sidekick Mark Robinson climb to the top of the mountain in mid-January and brave their way into a summit blizzard.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Blizzard

Video.summary: Dominica, the most volcanic and mountainous island in the Caribbean, is home to the Boiling Lake, a large waterfall-fed lake of furiously boiling water. Adventurer George Kourounis becomes the first person ever to traverse across the lake on a rope, risking his life to take water samples and shoot video from a place no human has ever been. He then dives to the ocean floor off the coast of the island to explore vents where the superheated water bubbles into the ocean.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Boiling Lake

Video.summary: One of the most exciting episodes of season one is “Wild Water” – our look at Great White Sharks, water-filled caves and high voltage marine action.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water Two

Video.summary: We hope Season Two will give us the opportunity to make the quintessential Angry Planet show – a full episode exploring a major hurricane that makes landfall in Florida or the Carolinas.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Hurricanes '07

Video.summary: 300 feet from a pocket of molten magma is a chamber filled with giant blue and pink crystals as 60 feet long!
Video.title: Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals

Image.alt: Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals
Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals
Angry Planet | Hot and Cold Crystals

Video.summary: George surfs with the “Hawaian Fire” firefighters.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Hawaii

Video.summary: Definitely our wildest episode so far. Here’s the deal: Mt. Elgon is an 14,000 foot volcano in eastern Kenya. We propose a safari/ascent of the dormant volcano. Here’s the first kicker: High on the the flanks of the volcano is Kitum Cave – a cave created by the most unique form of erosion – Elephant Erosion. For millennia elephants have been going into the cave to carve it out with their tusks in order to eat the salty rock. Here’s the second kicker: the cave is thought to be the home of the Ebola Marberg virus – the world’s most deadly virus. So here’s the plan: We are going enter the cave, at night, wearing full Bio-Hazard suits to protect us from the virus.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Kenya

Video.summary: George investigates the wild side of water in dangerous class 5 river rapids.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water

Video.summary: Mount Washington, New Hampshire has the dubious title of being home to the world’s worst weather. The highest wind ever recorded on the planet – 231 miles per hour – was recorded at its mountaintop observatory. To prepare for a mid-winter assault on the summit, Angry Planet host George Kourounis becomes a “human popsicle”, encased in ice in at the National Resarch Council’s high speed wind tunnel. Then he and his sidekick Mark Robinson climb to the top of the mountain in mid-January and brave their way into a summit blizzard.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Blizzard

Video.summary: Dominica, the most volcanic and mountainous island in the Caribbean, is home to the Boiling Lake, a large waterfall-fed lake of furiously boiling water. Adventurer George Kourounis becomes the first person ever to traverse across the lake on a rope, risking his life to take water samples and shoot video from a place no human has ever been. He then dives to the ocean floor off the coast of the island to explore vents where the superheated water bubbles into the ocean.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Boiling Lake

Video.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water Two

Video.title: Angry Planet | Hurricanes '07

Video.title: Angry Planet | Hawaii

Video.summary: Definitely our wildest episode so far. Here’s the deal: Mt. Elgon is an 14,000 foot volcano in eastern Kenya. We propose a safari/ascent of the dormant volcano. Here’s the first kicker: High on the the flanks of the volcano is Kitum Cave – a cave created by the most unique form of erosion – Elephant Erosion. For millennia elephants have been going into the cave to carve it out with their tusks in order to eat the salty rock. Here’s the second kicker: the cave is thought to be the home of the Ebola Marberg virus – the world’s most deadly virus. So here’s the plan: We are going enter the cave, at night, wearing full Bio-Hazard suits to protect us from the virus.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Kenya

Video.title: Angry Planet | Wild Water

Video.summary: Mount Washington, New Hampshire has the dubious title of being home to the world’s worst weather. The highest wind ever recorded on the planet – 231 miles per hour – was recorded at its mountaintop observatory. To prepare for a mid-winter assault on the summit, Angry Planet host George Kourounis becomes a “human popsicle”, encased in ice in at the National Resarch Council’s high speed wind tunnel. Then he and his sidekick Mark Robinson climb to the top of the mountain in mid-January and brave their way into a summit blizzard.

Adventurer George Kourounis becomes the first person ever to traverse across the lake on a rope, risking his life to take water samples and shoot video from a place no human has ever been. He then dives to the ocean floor off the coast of the island to explore vents where the superheated water bubbles into the ocean.

Video.title: Angry Planet | Hawaii

Video.summary: Definitely our wildest episode so far. Here’s the deal: Mt. Elgon is an 14,000 foot volcano in eastern Kenya. We propose a safari/ascent of the dormant volcano. Here’s the first kicker: High on the the flanks of the volcano is Kitum Cave – a cave created by the most unique form of erosion – Elephant Erosion. For millennia elephants have been going into the cave to carve it out with their tusks in order to eat the salty rock. Here’s the second kicker: the cave is thought to be the home of the Ebola Marberg virus – the world’s most deadly virus. So here’s the plan: We are going enter the cave, at night, wearing full Bio-Hazard suits to protect us from the virus.
Video.title: Angry Planet | Kenya

Video.summary: Durimg Super Typhoon Haiyan, Josh Morgerman found himself rescuing locals from its aftermath.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Super Typhoon Haiyan

Video.summary: Josh relives his most harrowing moments hunting the world’s most dangerous storms.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases

Image.alt: Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases
Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases
Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases

Video.summary: In this episode, we recount some of Josh Morgerman's greatest chases throughout the country of Mexico, which over the years became his favorite storm hunting grounds.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Mexico’s Megastorms

Video.summary: Josh Morgerman traveled to Texas to be an eye-witness to Hurricane Harvey.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Harvey

Video.summary: In a 1-year span, Hurricanes Laura and Ida hit one of the most hurricane prone regions of the United States.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Cajun Curse

Video.summary: One hurricane, 2 countries, 2,000 miles. Josh chases Hurricane Fiona from the Dominican Republic to Canada.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Fiona

Video.summary: Josh Morgerman chases devastating Hurricane Maria.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Maria

Video.summary: In 2014, Josh went face to face with one of the most violent hurricanes ever to strike Cabo San Lucas.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Odile

Video.summary: In 2019, Josh Morgerman chased Hurricane Dorian in the Bahamas in what wound up becoming one of the most intense and dangerous storms he has ever encountered.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Dorian

Video.summary: In this episode, we recount three of Josh Morgerman's most intense chases throughout the country of Taiwan, and witness his years of experience aid him in avoiding catastrophe a world away.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Taiwan

Video.summary: In 2022, Josh chased the deadliest hurricane to strike Florida since 1935: Category 4 Ian.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Ian

Video.summary: Josh Morgerman chased 3 typhoons in 6 days, a personal record amidst the Western Pacific's destructive forces.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hat Trick

Video.summary: In 2014, Josh went face to face with one of the most violent hurricanes ever to strike Cabo San Lucas.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Odile

Video.summary: One hurricane, 2 countries, 2,000 miles. Josh chases Hurricane Fiona from the Dominican Republic to Canada.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Fiona

Video.summary: In 2015, Josh Morgerman traveled to Mexico to chase what would become one of the strongest tropical hurricanes ever recorded. His harrowing experience with Patricia would forever shape his identity as a Hurricane Chaser.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Hurricane Patricia

Video.summary: Josh relives his most harrowing moments hunting the world’s most dangerous storms.
Video.title: Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases

Image.alt: Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases
Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases
Mission: Hurricane | Top 10 Most Dangerous Chases
